name: Replace DEFAULT_CONSENT_PURPOSE with granular purposes across the full consent workflow (parental grants, approve/revoke/restore, family-v2, profile-v2)
description: WI-1193 deliberately narrowed its delivered purpose split to adult self-consent. On origin/main@3ca714e0b63f02562084fc5d17c837972adf4d4d, the guardian/non-adult workflow still treats DEFAULT_CONSENT_PURPOSE='platform_use' as the whole consent relationship across requests, grants, approval, revoke/restore, status resolution, and downstream gates. Complete that split by applying the workflow-neutral named purpose set already introduced by WI-1193 (platform_use and llm_disclosure) consistently across the full non-adult consent workflow. Preserve lawful-basis, authority, org-scoping, audit, token-supersession, and append-only history semantics. Never infer a new-purpose grant without recorded versioned evidence. This is one consent-state-machine change, not supporter-visibility or family-join remediation; do not claim it concurrently with WI-2127 or WI-2128, and re-read/rebase their landed surfaces if either completes first.
acceptanceCriteria:
1. Purpose-set contract: one workflow-neutral, typed source of truth P defines every named purpose required by the guardian/non-adult consent workflow, initially the same independently recorded purposes WI-1193 established for adult self-consent: platform_use and llm_disclosure. Production code must not express whole-workflow intent through DEFAULT_CONSENT_PURPOSE, an omitted/defaulted purpose argument, or a literal platform_use proxy. Adding a later purpose changes P once and makes every set-based path include it.

2. Consent-event writes: for every production event that creates or approves guardian/non-adult consent (pending/direct creation, emailed request and response, family/profile creation, and every sibling discovered by a whole-repo scan), the request representation names the intended purpose set and a successful approval/grant persists one current, separately auditable consent_grant fact per member of P. The set commits atomically: no partial request/grant set survives an error or concurrent retry, while the existing per-basis idempotency and resend/recipient caps remain intact.

3. Withdrawal and restore: purpose-aware service primitives select an explicit purpose. A single-purpose operation changes only that purpose. Existing guardian whole-consent revoke/restore routes and the verified bearer-token withdrawal apply to the complete purpose set tied to that consent event atomically, retain their current authority and token-supersession checks, preserve append-only restore history, and do not leave an accidental mixed state. Independent adult self-withdrawal continues to affect only its requested purpose.

4. Read/gate semantics: every production consumer whose answer currently depends on DEFAULT_CONSENT_PURPOSE or literal platform_use is converted by property, not by a fixed file count. A consumer asking about one purpose passes it explicitly; a consumer asking whether the overall guardian/non-adult consent relationship is satisfied uses one shared aggregate over P. The aggregate returns CONSENTED only when every required purpose has a current unwithdrawn grant; a missing, pending, denied, or withdrawn member cannot be masked by another consented member. Family/profile/status/dashboard reads, LLM and background-processing gates, reminder/revocation/archive/deletion flows, accountability/export surfaces, and SQL scan predicates agree on that result.

5. Existing-data safety: ship a forward migration or an explicit re-consent path for pre-change consent_request/consent_grant rows. A purpose grant may be derived only when the stored, versioned request/grant audit evidence proves that purpose was covered; otherwise it remains ungranted and the user is driven through normal consent. No migration, seed, repair, database default, or runtime fallback may fabricate llm_disclosure consent from a platform_use row or a bare timestamp. Rollback/data-loss handling is documented per the repo migration rules.

6. Contract and regression guard: service constants, @eduagent/schemas wire contracts, Drizzle schema/defaults, migrations, test seeds, and API response/request shapes agree with P. An executable whole-repo guard fails if new production code reintroduces DEFAULT_CONSENT_PURPOSE, an implicit database purpose default, or literal platform_use as a whole-consent selector; historical migrations and evidence fixtures are allowed only through a narrow documented allowlist. Adult self-consent/accountability behavior and the GDPR-vs-COPPA basis isolation remain unchanged.

7. Regression matrix: red-green integration coverage proves, for both direct guardian grants and emailed/token approval, that every member of P is written; one-purpose withdrawal cannot be hidden by another consented purpose; whole-consent revoke, bearer withdrawal, and restore cover the complete set; missing-purpose legacy data fails closed without fabricated grants; mixed-purpose family/profile/list/dashboard/processing reads agree; retries and concurrent approval/revoke/restore do not create partial or duplicate current state; and cross-person/cross-org isolation still holds. Extend the affected consent-v2, consent-status-v2, profile-v2, dashboard/family, solo-report, reminder, deletion, route, schema, and migration test surfaces found by the implementation sweep rather than satisfying a raw file count.

8. Verification method: run bash scripts/check-change-class.sh --run from the eduagent-build root and explicitly record green results for pnpm exec nx run api:lint, pnpm exec nx run api:typecheck, pnpm exec nx run api:test, pnpm exec nx run api:integration-api, and pnpm exec nx run api:test:integration. The completion evidence includes the origin/main SHA, the production-call-site scan before and after, the migration/re-consent disposition, and the red-green-revert proof for the new regression matrix.
riskImpact: P1 launch/compliance risk. A single-purpose proxy can incorrectly treat one platform-use grant as authorization for a separately revocable LLM-disclosure purpose, let a withdrawn/missing purpose be masked by another consented row, or make family/profile/background consumers disagree. A careless backfill would fabricate consent; a partial multi-purpose write or inconsistent restore could strand or wrongly process a charge. The change spans the consent state machine, database/wire contracts, and access-processing gates, and overlaps WI-2127/WI-2128 family-join work. Mitigate with Assisted execution, atomic set semantics, evidence-only migration/re-consent, property-defined call-site coverage, red-green-revert integration evidence, and Adversarial review.
bodyText: Scope-split from WI-1193 (4th-review finding #2, 2026-07-18): 1193 delivers granular purposes on the adult-self-consent path (verified landed); this WI completes the global replacement — every consent-writing surface (parental grants, approve/revoke/restore, family-v2, profile-v2) moves off the hardcoded DEFAULT_CONSENT_PURPOSE to the named granular purposes. COORDINATION WARNING: overlaps live supporter/visibility work (BID-16, esp. WI-2237) and family-join consent items (WI-2127/2128) — sequence via PM at formation, do not run concurrently with those unruled. Wired as Blocked-by input on WI-1577 (launch compliance closure) so purpose handling is checked at the gate. Route at triage: Compliance-Eng (natural BID-20-successor candidate). Tests at execution must name per-surface cases (each consent-writing call site: which purpose set is persisted). — pm:claude:mentomate
